req.title: Data retention (boundary)
req.statement: Resources shall be retained per policy; expired resources shall be purged on a nightly job with full audit trail. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The retention cron endpoint must purge expired resources and write an audit trail. We cover: (1) EP-positive: a clearly expired resource is purged and audited; (2) NEG-negative: a non-expired resource is NOT purged and no spurious audit entry is written; (3) BVA-boundary: a resource whose expiry timestamp equals exactly midnight of the nightly-job run (the boundary between retained and expired) is treated as expired; (4) STC: state transition from 'active' through 'expired' to 'purged' is observable via audit log; (5) DT: policy mismatch raises an incident. Each test seeds its own data, triggers the cron, and asserts outcomes independently.

tests.name: test_expired_resource_is_purged_and_audited
preconditions: Auth token with cron-trigger scope is available
A resource with expiry_date 30 days in the past exists in the system
actions: Seed a resource with expiry_date = today - 30 days via <seed_resource_fixture>
Query GET /audit-log for the resource ID
expected: Audit log contains an entry with action='PURGE' for the resource ID
trace: R-HLT-045: expired resources shall be purged on nightly job
R-HLT-045: full audit trail must be written on purge
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import date, timedelta

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"


def test_expired_resource_is_purged_and_audited(<cron_auth_header>, <seed_resource_fixture>):
    expiry = (date.today() - timedelta(days=30)).isoformat()
    resource_id = <seed_resource_fixture>(expiry_date=expiry)

    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/cron/retention",
        headers=<cron_auth_header>,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    fetch = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/resources/{resource_id}",
        headers=<cron_auth_header>,
        timeout=5,
    )
    assert fetch.status_code == 404, "Expired resource must be purged"

    audit = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/audit-log",
        params={"resource_id": resource_id},
        headers=<cron_auth_header>,
        timeout=5,
    ).json()
    actions = [e["action"] for e in audit.get("entries", [])]
    assert "PURGE" in actions, "Audit log must record PURGE action"

tests.name: test_active_resource_is_not_purged
preconditions: Auth token with cron-trigger scope is available
A resource with expiry_date 30 days in the future exists in the system
actions: Seed a resource with expiry_date = today + 30 days via <seed_resource_fixture>
Query GET /resources/{id} to confirm resource still exists
Query GET /audit-log to confirm no PURGE entry for the resource
expected: Audit log contains NO entry with action='PURGE' for the resource ID
trace: R-HLT-045: only expired resources shall be purged
R-HLT-045: error pathway — incorrect purge would be a policy mismatch
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import date, timedelta

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"


def test_active_resource_is_not_purged(<cron_auth_header>, <seed_resource_fixture>, <cleanup_fixture>):
    expiry = (date.today() + timedelta(days=30)).isoformat()
    resource_id = <seed_resource_fixture>(expiry_date=expiry)
    <cleanup_fixture>.register(resource_id)

    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/cron/retention",
        headers=<cron_auth_header>,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    fetch = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/resources/{resource_id}",
        headers=<cron_auth_header>,
        timeout=5,
    )
    assert fetch.status_code == 200, "Active resource must NOT be purged"

    audit = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/audit-log",
        params={"resource_id": resource_id},
        headers=<cron_auth_header>,
        timeout=5,
    ).json()
    actions = [e["action"] for e in audit.get("entries", [])]
    assert "PURGE" not in actions, "No PURGE audit entry expected for active resource"

tests.name: test_resource_expiring_exactly_at_midnight_boundary_is_purged
preconditions: Auth token with cron-trigger scope is available
A resource with expiry_date equal to today's date (midnight boundary) exists
actions: Seed a resource with expiry_date = today (exactly the boundary date) via <seed_resource_fixture>
expected: GET /resources/{id} returns HTTP 404 (on-boundary resource is treated as expired)
Audit log contains an entry with action='PURGE' for the resource ID
trace: R-HLT-045: boundary cases on timestamps must be tested
R-HLT-045: policy defines expiry_date == job_run_date as expired
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import date

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"


def test_resource_expiring_exactly_at_midnight_boundary_is_purged(<cron_auth_header>, <seed_resource_fixture>):
    expiry = date.today().isoformat()  # boundary: exactly today
    resource_id = <seed_resource_fixture>(expiry_date=expiry)

    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/cron/retention",
        headers=<cron_auth_header>,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    fetch = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/resources/{resource_id}",
        headers=<cron_auth_header>,
        timeout=5,
    )
    assert fetch.status_code == 404, "Boundary-expiry resource must be purged"

    audit = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/audit-log",
        params={"resource_id": resource_id},
        headers=<cron_auth_header>,
        timeout=5,
    ).json()
    actions = [e["action"] for e in audit.get("entries", [])]
    assert "PURGE" in actions, "Boundary-expiry purge must appear in audit log"

tests.name: test_purge_state_transition_active_to_expired_to_purged
preconditions: Auth token with cron-trigger scope is available
A resource can be seeded with a controllable expiry_date
actions: Seed resource with expiry_date = today - 1 day (state: expired but not yet purged)
Assert resource exists and its status is 'expired' before cron run
Assert resource no longer exists (state: purged)
Assert audit log captures each state transition event
expected: Before cron: GET /resources/{id} returns 200 with status='expired'
Audit log shows transition entries: 'EXPIRED' then 'PURGE'
trace: R-HLT-045: state transition from active -> expired -> purged must be observable
R-HLT-045: audit trail must reflect all transitions
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import date, timedelta

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"


def test_purge_state_transition_active_to_expired_to_purged(<cron_auth_header>, <seed_resource_fixture>):
    expiry = (date.today() - timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    resource_id = <seed_resource_fixture>(expiry_date=expiry)

    pre = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/resources/{resource_id}",
        headers=<cron_auth_header>,
        timeout=5,
    ).json()
    assert pre.get("status") == "expired", "Resource must be in expired state before purge"

    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/cron/retention",
        headers=<cron_auth_header>,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    post = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/resources/{resource_id}",
        headers=<cron_auth_header>,
        timeout=5,
    )
    assert post.status_code == 404, "Resource must be purged after cron"

    audit_entries = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/audit-log",
        params={"resource_id": resource_id},
        headers=<cron_auth_header>,
        timeout=5,
    ).json().get("entries", [])
    audit_actions = [e["action"] for e in audit_entries]
    assert "PURGE" in audit_actions, "PURGE transition must be audited"

tests.name: test_policy_mismatch_raises_incident
preconditions: Auth token with cron-trigger scope is available
A resource exists whose policy metadata is inconsistent (e.g. policy_id references a deleted policy)
actions: Seed a resource with an invalid or mismatched policy_id via <seed_resource_fixture>
Query GET /incidents for an entry referencing the resource
expected: POST /cron/retention returns HTTP 200 (job completes; errors are logged, not fatal)
GET /incidents returns at least one incident with resource_id and reason='POLICY_MISMATCH'
Resource is NOT purged (left intact pending manual review)
trace: R-HLT-045: error pathway — incident raised on policy mismatch
R-HLT-045: decision table: mismatched policy -> incident, no purge
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import date, timedelta

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"
INVALID_POLICY_ID = "nonexistent-policy-99999"


def test_policy_mismatch_raises_incident(<cron_auth_header>, <seed_resource_fixture>, <cleanup_fixture>):
    expiry = (date.today() - timedelta(days=5)).isoformat()
    resource_id = <seed_resource_fixture>(
        expiry_date=expiry,
        policy_id=INVALID_POLICY_ID,
    )
    <cleanup_fixture>.register(resource_id)

    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/cron/retention",
        headers=<cron_auth_header>,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200, "Cron job must complete even with mismatch"

    fetch = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/resources/{resource_id}",
        headers=<cron_auth_header>,
        timeout=5,
    )
    assert fetch.status_code == 200, "Mismatched resource must NOT be auto-purged"

    incidents = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/incidents",
        params={"resource_id": resource_id},
        headers=<cron_auth_header>,
        timeout=5,
    ).json().get("incidents", [])
    reasons = [i["reason"] for i in incidents]
    assert "POLICY_MISMATCH" in reasons, "Incident with POLICY_MISMATCH must be raised"
